title: Goroutine Pools, Context Causes, Backpressure, and Shutdown · Stakeholder Go Mission
summary: A consortium of small public documentation owners. They need streaming sitemap and semantic link discovery, trap containment, deterministic frontier state, per-origin fairness, bounded goroutines, cancellation, and leak-free shutdown. Planned artifact: Deliver Race-Safe Fair Documentation Site Graph with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence…
objectives: Model sitemap indexes as a bounded directed graph with unique identities, depth limits, cycle detection, and stable traversal.
Store source and target identity, element, attribute, raw value, relation, scope decision, and observation separately from fetch state.
Reserve admitted URL keys atomically before enqueue so concurrent discoveries cannot duplicate effects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Sitemap Index Graph
steps.instruction:
project case — A project Go team handles case 5729 during a malformed HTML and parser differential clinic. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove multiresource backpressure, channel ownership, context causes, error aggregation, and graceful join; owner permission, robots preference, and legal-review conclusions stay distinct; then change one content version, metadata, accessibility, resource, browser-service, or report condition without overclaiming. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?
